acceptance_criteria.text: The audit proves how this repository talks to the OpenAI-compatible proxy today: which endpoint is called, which request fields are sent for native responses, which local health/model probes are used from the repo root, and whether that transport matches the current documented Responses API shape for conversation continuation and compaction.
proof: src/agent/native-responses.ts sends requests to POST {OPENAI_BASE_URL}/responses and forwards previous_response_id and context_management.
src/smoke.ts probes /health and /v1/models for a local proxy URL, and deploy/systemd/eveai-codex-proxy.service points the local proxy boundary at /home/antipedik/codex_proxy_v2 on port 8088.
/home/antipedik/codex_proxy_v2/src/main.rs resolves previous_response_id locally and auto-injects context_management when the client omits it.
OpenAI API changelog documents context management using compaction and the /v1/responses/compact endpoint.
.agent/tasks/codex-proxy-v2-audit/raw/smoke.txt shows live proxy health and models responses from 127.0.0.1:8088.
gaps: npm run smoke failed its app_health leg because localhost:8000 was not serving during evidence capture; proxy boundary checks still passed.

acceptance_criteria.text: The audit proves whether previous_id / previous_response_id is used correctly for history continuation in the current implementation: within a single tool loop, across separate user turns, and at the persistence boundary (agent_threads.last_response_id). If the implementation is inconsistent with the intended model or with current OpenAI documentation, the code and docs are corrected minimally.
proof: src/agent/executor.ts now plans warm versus cold continuation before each turn.
src/agent/executor.ts uses a fresh agent_threads.last_response_id as previous_response_id for warm turns and falls back to SQLite-backed history for stale or missing state.
src/agent/executor.ts persists the final response id on success and clears it on error, fallback, or timeout.
tests/unit/warm-cold-path.test.ts covers both the warm and stale cold-start paths.
.agent/tasks/codex-proxy-v2-audit/raw/test-unit.txt shows the focused unit suite passed.

acceptance_criteria.text: The audit proves whether compaction is used correctly in both places the code supports it: server-managed Responses API compaction via context_management / compact_threshold, and local SQLite thread summarization via compactThreadIfNeeded. The final state must make the interaction between these mechanisms explicit and internally consistent.
proof: src/agent/executor.ts sends context_management when OPENAI_COMPACT_THRESHOLD is configured.
src/agent/compact.ts still handles local SQLite summarization and pruning for cold-start fallback.
tests/unit/native-responses.test.ts verifies the request body contains previous_response_id and context_management.
tests/unit/compact.test.ts verifies summary creation and message pruning.

/home/antipedik/codex_proxy_v2/src/main.rs exposes POST /v1/responses/compact, while the application currently relies on automatic context_management plus local summary fallback instead of calling it directly.

acceptance_criteria.text: The repository includes focused regression coverage and evidence for the proxy-v2 path from the repo root, covering request payload construction, continuation behavior, and compaction-related behavior without exposing secrets or depending on undocumented proxy internals.
proof: .agent/tasks/codex-proxy-v2-audit/raw/test-unit.txt shows focused unit coverage passed.
.agent/tasks/codex-proxy-v2-audit/raw/test-integration.txt shows integration coverage passed.
.agent/tasks/codex-proxy-v2-audit/raw/build.txt and raw/lint.txt show build, lint, and typecheck passed.
.agent/tasks/codex-proxy-v2-audit/raw/smoke.txt captures live proxy checks from this repository.

acceptance_criteria.text: The repository documentation and task artifacts state one consistent conclusion about proxy-v2 communication, previous_id history handling, and compaction, so a fresh verifier can judge the current codebase without relying on chat history.
proof: ARCHITECTURE.md documents the warm/cold continuation path in the Telegram request flow.
docs/RELIABILITY.md documents fresh-only previous_response_id continuation and cold fallback behavior.
.agent/tasks/codex-proxy-v2-audit/spec.md and evidence.md describe the same runtime model.

commands_for_fresh_verifier: npm run test -- tests/unit/warm-cold-path.test.ts tests/unit/native-responses.test.ts tests/unit/compact.test.ts
known_gaps: npm run smoke failed its app_health leg because localhost:8000 was not serving during evidence capture; proxy boundary checks still passed.
